{{Use mdy dates|date=October 2020}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Samuraizuka Kofun | native_name =侍塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Shimosamuraizuka Kofun 2.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Shimo-Samuraizuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Tochigi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Shimo-Samuraizuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|48|47.51|N|140|7|22.17|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Ōtawara, Tochigi]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kantō region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 4th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Samuraizuka Kofun'''|侍塚古墳 |}} is a pair of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]]s located in the Yuzukami neighborhood of the city of [[Ōtawara, Tochigi|Ōtawara]] in [[Tochigi Prefecture]] in the northern [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. Both received protection as a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 1951.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/215989|title=侍塚古墳 |trans-title= Fujimoto Kannonyama Kofun |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref> The neighborhood has many smaller ''kofun'', including one [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped tumulus]], seven {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=dome-shaped|ja|円墳}} tumuli and one square-shaped tumulus, which are covered under an Ōtawara City Historic Site designation.
==Overview== The Samuraizuka Kofun are both {{nihongo|"two conjoined rectangles" shaped|前方後方墳 |zenpō-kōhō-fun}}. They are located on the right bank of the [[Naka River (Tochigi Ibaraki)|Naka River]] in the northern end of the [[Kanto Plain]]. The {{nihongo|'''Shimo-Samuraizuka Kofun'''|下侍塚古墳||}} is to the north, and the {{nihongo|'''Kami-Samuraizuka Kofun'''|上侍塚古墳||}} is located some 700 meters to the south. The ''kofun'' were excavated in 1692 by order of [[Tokugawa Mitsukuni]], who kept a detailed illustrated records of the artifacts discovered. The artifacts were later placed in boxes made from [[Japanese red pine]] restored to the [[burial chamber]]s, and the site was backfilled. Tokugawa Mitsukuni attributed the tombs to that of the Nasu ''[[Kuni no miyatsuko]]'' named "Ataiide" mentioned on a stele dated 689 AD discovered in a shrine in Ōtawara City, which lists the names of prehistoric rulers from the 5th century.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The Kamisamuraizuka Kofunis the larger tumulus, with a total length of 114 meters. Artifacts included [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirrors]], fragments of armor, cylindrical beads and iron balls.
;Overall length: 114 meters ;Posterior portion: 60 meters wide x 12 meters high ;Anterior portion: 52 meters wide x 7 meters high
The Shimosamuraizuka has a total length of 84 meters, and was surrounded by a moat. Artifacts included bronze mirrors, iron swords and [[Haji ware]] pottery. In 1975, the Shimo-Samuraizuka was [[archaeological excavation|trench excavated]] in connection with a land improvement project and more Haji-ware shards were discovered.
;Overall length: 84 meters ;Posterior portion: 48 meters wide x 9.4 meters high ;Anterior portion: 36 meters wide x 5 meters high
These tumuli are considered to have been built around the beginning of the 5th century AD, with the Shimosamuraizuka being the older of the two. They are located a short walk from the "Sajizuka Parking Lot" bus stop on the municipal bus from [[Nasushiobara Station]] on the [[Tōhoku Shinkansen]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Use mdy dates|date=October 2020}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Samuraizuka Kofun | native_name =侍塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Shimosamuraizuka Kofun 2.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Shimo-Samuraizuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Tochigi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Shimo-Samuraizuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|48|47.51|N|140|7|22.17|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Ōtawara, Tochigi]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kantō region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 4th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Samuraizuka Kofun'''|侍塚古墳 |}} is a pair of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]]s located in the Yuzukami neighborhood of the city of [[Ōtawara, Tochigi|Ōtawara]] in [[Tochigi Prefecture]] in the northern [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. Both received protection as a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 1951.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/215989|title=侍塚古墳 |trans-title= Fujimoto Kannonyama Kofun |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref> The neighborhood has many smaller ''kofun'', including one [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped tumulus]], seven {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=dome-shaped|ja|円墳}} tumuli and one square-shaped tumulus, which are covered under an Ōtawara City Historic Site designation.
==Overview== The Samuraizuka Kofun are both {{nihongo|"two conjoined rectangles" shaped|前方後方墳 |zenpō-kōhō-fun}}. They are located on the right bank of the [[Naka River (Tochigi Ibaraki)|Naka River]] in the northern end of the [[Kanto Plain]]. The {{nihongo|'''Shimo-Samuraizuka Kofun'''|下侍塚古墳||}} is to the north, and the {{nihongo|'''Kami-Samuraizuka Kofun'''|上侍塚古墳||}} is located some 700 meters to the south. The ''kofun'' were excavated in 1692 by order of [[Tokugawa Mitsukuni]], who kept a detailed illustrated records of the artifacts discovered. The artifacts were later placed in boxes made from [[Japanese red pine]] restored to the [[burial chamber]]s, and the site was backfilled. Tokugawa Mitsukuni attributed the tombs to that of the Nasu ''[[Kuni no miyatsuko]]'' named "Ataiide" mentioned on a stele dated 689 AD discovered in a shrine in Ōtawara City, which lists the names of prehistoric rulers from the 5th century.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The Kamisamuraizuka Kofunis the larger tumulus, with a total length of 114 meters. Artifacts included [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirrors]], fragments of armor, cylindrical beads and iron balls.
;Overall length: 114 meters ;Posterior portion: 60 meters wide x 12 meters high ;Anterior portion: 52 meters wide x 7 meters high
The Shimosamuraizuka has a total length of 84 meters, and was surrounded by a moat. Artifacts included bronze mirrors, iron swords and [[Haji ware]] pottery. In 1975, the Shimo-Samuraizuka was [[archaeological excavation|trench excavated]] in connection with a land improvement project and more Haji-ware shards were discovered.
;Overall length: 84 meters ;Posterior portion: 48 meters wide x 9.4 meters high ;Anterior portion: 36 meters wide x 5 meters high
These tumuli are considered to have been built around the beginning of the 5th century AD, with the Shimosamuraizuka being the older of the two. They are located a short walk from the "Sajizuka Parking Lot" bus stop on the municipal bus from [[Nasushiobara Station]] on the [[Tōhoku Shinkansen]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Use mdy dates|date=October 2020}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Samuraizuka Kofun | native_name =侍塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Shimosamuraizuka Kofun 2.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Shimo-Samuraizuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Tochigi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Shimo-Samuraizuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|48|47.51|N|140|7|22.17|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Ōtawara, Tochigi]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kantō region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 4th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Samuraizuka Kofun'''|侍塚古墳 |}} is a pair of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]]s located in the Yuzukami neighborhood of the city of [[Ōtawara, Tochigi|Ōtawara]] in [[Tochigi Prefecture]] in the northern [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. Both received protection as a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 1951.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/215989|title=侍塚古墳 |trans-title= Fujimoto Kannonyama Kofun |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref> The neighborhood has many smaller ''kofun'', including one [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped tumulus]], seven {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=dome-shaped|ja|円墳}} tumuli and one square-shaped tumulus, which are covered under an Ōtawara City Historic Site designation.
==Overview== The Samuraizuka Kofun are both {{nihongo|"two conjoined rectangles" shaped|前方後方墳 |zenpō-kōhō-fun}}. They are located on the right bank of the [[Naka River (Tochigi Ibaraki)|Naka River]] in the northern end of the [[Kanto Plain]]. The {{nihongo|'''Shimo-Samuraizuka Kofun'''|下侍塚古墳||}} is to the north, and the {{nihongo|'''Kami-Samuraizuka Kofun'''|上侍塚古墳||}} is located some 700 meters to the south. The ''kofun'' were excavated in 1692 by order of [[Tokugawa Mitsukuni]], who kept a detailed illustrated records of the artifacts discovered. The artifacts were later placed in boxes made from [[Japanese red pine]] restored to the [[burial chamber]]s, and the site was backfilled. Tokugawa Mitsukuni attributed the tombs to that of the Nasu ''[[Kuni no miyatsuko]]'' named "Ataiide" mentioned on a stele dated 689 AD discovered in a shrine in Ōtawara City, which lists the names of prehistoric rulers from the 5th century.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The Kamisamuraizuka Kofunis the larger tumulus, with a total length of 114 meters. Artifacts included [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirrors]], fragments of armor, cylindrical beads and iron balls.
;Overall length: 114 meters ;Posterior portion: 60 meters wide x 12 meters high ;Anterior portion: 52 meters wide x 7 meters high
The Shimosamuraizuka has a total length of 84 meters, and was surrounded by a moat. Artifacts included bronze mirrors, iron swords and [[Haji ware]] pottery. In 1975, the Shimo-Samuraizuka was [[archaeological excavation|trench excavated]] in connection with a land improvement project and more Haji-ware shards were discovered.
;Overall length: 84 meters ;Posterior portion: 48 meters wide x 9.4 meters high ;Anterior portion: 36 meters wide x 5 meters high
These tumuli are considered to have been built around the beginning of the 5th century AD, with the Shimosamuraizuka being the older of the two. They are located a short walk from the "Sajizuka Parking Lot" bus stop on the municipal bus from [[Nasushiobara Station]] on the [[Tōhoku Shinkansen]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== {{Portal|History|Japan}} *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Tochigi)]]

{{Use mdy dates|date=October 2020}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Samuraizuka Kofun | native_name =侍塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Shimosamuraizuka Kofun 2.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Shimo-Samuraizuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Tochigi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Shimo-Samuraizuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|48|47.51|N|140|7|22.17|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Ōtawara, Tochigi]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kantō region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 4th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Samuraizuka Kofun'''|侍塚古墳 |}} is a pair of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]]s located in the Yuzukami neighborhood of the city of [[Ōtawara, Tochigi|Ōtawara]] in [[Tochigi Prefecture]] in the northern [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. Both received protection as a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 1951.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/215989|title=侍塚古墳 |trans-title= Fujimoto Kannonyama Kofun |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref> The neighborhood has many smaller ''kofun'', including one [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped tumulus]], seven {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=dome-shaped|ja|円墳}} tumuli and one square-shaped tumulus, which are covered under an Ōtawara City Historic Site designation.
==Overview== The Samuraizuka Kofun are both {{nihongo|"two conjoined rectangles" shaped|前方後方墳 |zenpō-kōhō-fun}}. They are located on the right bank of the [[Naka River (Tochigi Ibaraki)|Naka River]] in the northern end of the [[Kanto Plain]]. The {{nihongo|'''Shimo-Samuraizuka Kofun'''|下侍塚古墳||}} is to the north, and the {{nihongo|'''Kami-Samuraizuka Kofun'''|上侍塚古墳||}} is located some 700 meters to the south. The ''kofun'' were excavated in 1692 by order of [[Tokugawa Mitsukuni]], who kept a detailed illustrated records of the artifacts discovered. The artifacts were later placed in boxes made from [[Japanese red pine]] restored to the [[burial chamber]]s, and the site was backfilled. Tokugawa Mitsukuni attributed the tombs to that of the Nasu ''[[Kuni no miyatsuko]]'' named "Ataiide" mentioned on a stele dated 689 AD discovered in a shrine in Ōtawara City, which lists the names of prehistoric rulers from the 5th century.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The Kamisamuraizuka Kofunis the larger tumulus, with a total length of 114 meters. Artifacts included [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirrors]], fragments of armor, cylindrical beads and iron balls.
;Overall length: 114 meters ;Posterior portion: 60 meters wide x 12 meters high ;Anterior portion: 52 meters wide x 7 meters high
The Shimosamuraizuka has a total length of 84 meters, and was surrounded by a moat. Artifacts included bronze mirrors, iron swords and [[Haji ware]] pottery. In 1975, the Shimo-Samuraizuka was [[archaeological excavation|trench excavated]] in connection with a land improvement project and more Haji-ware shards were discovered.
;Overall length: 84 meters ;Posterior portion: 48 meters wide x 9.4 meters high ;Anterior portion: 36 meters wide x 5 meters high
These tumuli are considered to have been built around the beginning of the 5th century AD, with the Shimosamuraizuka being the older of the two. They are located a short walk from the "Sajizuka Parking Lot" bus stop on the municipal bus from [[Nasushiobara Station]] on the [[Tōhoku Shinkansen]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== {{Portal|History|Japan}} *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Tochigi)]]

{{Use mdy dates|date=October 2020}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Samuraizuka Kofun | native_name =侍塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Shimosamuraizuka Kofun 2.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Shimo-Samuraizuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Tochigi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Shimo-Samuraizuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|48|47.51|N|140|7|22.17|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Ōtawara, Tochigi]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kantō region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 4th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Samuraizuka Kofun'''|侍塚古墳 |}} is a pair of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]]s located in the Yuzukami neighborhood of the city of [[Ōtawara, Tochigi|Ōtawara]] in [[Tochigi Prefecture]] in the northern [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. Both received protection as a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 1951.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/215989|title=侍塚古墳 |trans-title= Fujimoto Kannonyama Kofun |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref> The neighborhood has many smaller ''kofun'', including one [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped tumulus]], seven {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=dome-shaped|ja|円墳}} tumuli and one square-shaped tumulus, which are covered under an Ōtawara City Historic Site designation.
==Overview== The Samuraizuka Kofun are both {{nihongo|"two conjoined rectangles" shaped|前方後方墳 |zenpō-kōhō-fun}}. They are located on the right bank of the [[Naka River (Tochigi Ibaraki)|Naka River]] in the northern end of the [[Kanto Plain]]. The {{nihongo|'''Shimo-Samuraizuka Kofun'''|下侍塚古墳||}} is to the north, and the {{nihongo|'''Kami-Samuraizuka Kofun'''|上侍塚古墳||}} is located some 700 meters to the south. The ''kofun'' were excavated in 1692 by order of [[Tokugawa Mitsukuni]], who kept a detailed illustrated records of the artifacts discovered. The artifacts were later placed in boxes made from [[Japanese red pine]] restored to the [[burial chamber]]s, and the site was backfilled. Tokugawa Mitsukuni attributed the tombs to that of the Nasu ''[[Kuni no miyatsuko]]'' named "Ataiide" mentioned on a stele dated 689 AD discovered in a shrine in Ōtawara City, which lists the names of prehistoric rulers from the 5th century.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The Kamisamuraizuka Kofunis the larger tumulus, with a total length of 114 meters. Artifacts included [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirrors]], fragments of armor, cylindrical beads and iron balls.
;Overall length: 114 meters ;Posterior portion: 60 meters wide x 12 meters high ;Anterior portion: 52 meters wide x 7 meters high
The Shimosamuraizuka has a total length of 84 meters, and was surrounded by a moat. Artifacts included bronze mirrors, iron swords and [[Haji ware]] pottery. In 1975, the Shimo-Samuraizuka was [[archaeological excavation|trench excavated]] in connection with a land improvement project and more Haji-ware shards were discovered.
;Overall length: 84 meters ;Posterior portion: 48 meters wide x 9.4 meters high ;Anterior portion: 36 meters wide x 5 meters high
These tumuli are considered to have been built around the beginning of the 5th century AD, with the Shimosamuraizuka being the older of the two. They are located a short walk from the "Sajizuka Parking Lot" bus stop on the municipal bus from [[Nasushiobara Station]] on the [[Tōhoku Shinkansen]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== {{Portal|History|Japan}} *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Tochigi)]]

{{Use mdy dates|date=October 2020}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Samuraizuka Kofun | native_name =侍塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Shimosamuraizuka Kofun 2.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Shimo-Samuraizuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Tochigi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Shimo-Samuraizuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|48|47.51|N|140|7|22.17|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Ōtawara, Tochigi]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kantō region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 4th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Samuraizuka Kofun'''|侍塚古墳 |}} is a pair of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]]s located in the Yuzukami neighborhood of the city of [[Ōtawara, Tochigi|Ōtawara]] in [[Tochigi Prefecture]] in the northern [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. Both received protection as a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 1951.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/215989|title=侍塚古墳 |trans-title= Fujimoto Kannonyama Kofun |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref> The neighborhood has many smaller ''kofun'', including one [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped tumulus]], seven {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=dome-shaped|ja|円墳}} tumuli and one square-shaped tumulus, which are covered under an Ōtawara City Historic Site designation.
==Overview== The Samuraizuka Kofun are both {{nihongo|"two conjoined rectangles" shaped|前方後方墳 |zenpō-kōhō-fun}}. They are located on the right bank of the [[Naka River (Tochigi Ibaraki)|Naka River]] in the northern end of the [[Kanto Plain]]. The {{nihongo|'''Shimo-Samuraizuka Kofun'''|下侍塚古墳||}} is to the north, and the {{nihongo|'''Kami-Samuraizuka Kofun'''|上侍塚古墳||}} is located some 700 meters to the south. The ''kofun'' were excavated in 1692 by order of [[Tokugawa Mitsukuni]], who kept a detailed illustrated records of the artifacts discovered. The artifacts were later placed in boxes made from [[Japanese red pine]] restored to the [[burial chamber]]s, and the site was backfilled. Tokugawa Mitsukuni attributed the tombs to that of the Nasu ''[[Kuni no miyatsuko]]'' named "Ataiide" mentioned on a stele dated 689 AD discovered in a shrine in Ōtawara City, which lists the names of prehistoric rulers from the 5th century.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The Kamisamuraizuka Kofunis the larger tumulus, with a total length of 114 meters. Artifacts included [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirrors]], fragments of armor, cylindrical beads and iron balls.
;Overall length: 114 meters ;Posterior portion: 60 meters wide x 12 meters high ;Anterior portion: 52 meters wide x 7 meters high
The Shimosamuraizuka has a total length of 84 meters, and was surrounded by a moat. Artifacts included bronze mirrors, iron swords and [[Haji ware]] pottery. In 1975, the Shimo-Samuraizuka was [[archaeological excavation|trench excavated]] in connection with a land improvement project and more Haji-ware shards were discovered.
;Overall length: 84 meters ;Posterior portion: 48 meters wide x 9.4 meters high ;Anterior portion: 36 meters wide x 5 meters high
These tumuli are considered to have been built around the beginning of the 5th century AD, with the Shimosamuraizuka being the older of the two. They are located a short walk from the "Sajizuka Parking Lot" bus stop on the municipal bus from [[Nasushiobara Station]] on the [[Tōhoku Shinkansen]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== {{Portal|History|Japan}} *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Tochigi)]]

{{Use mdy dates|date=October 2020}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Samuraizuka Kofun | native_name =侍塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Shimosamuraizuka Kofun 2.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Shimo-Samuraizuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Tochigi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Shimo-Samuraizuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|48|47.51|N|140|7|22.17|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Ōtawara, Tochigi]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kantō region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 4th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Samuraizuka Kofun'''|侍塚古墳 |}} is a pair of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]]s located in the Yuzukami neighborhood of the city of [[Ōtawara, Tochigi|Ōtawara]] in [[Tochigi Prefecture]] in the northern [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. Both received protection as a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 1951.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/215989|title=侍塚古墳 |trans-title= Fujimoto Kannonyama Kofun |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref> The neighborhood has many smaller ''kofun'', including one [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped tumulus]], seven {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=dome-shaped|ja|円墳}} tumuli and one square-shaped tumulus, which are covered under an Ōtawara City Historic Site designation.
==Overview== The Samuraizuka Kofun are both {{nihongo|"two conjoined rectangles" shaped|前方後方墳 |zenpō-kōhō-fun}}. They are located on the right bank of the [[Naka River (Tochigi Ibaraki)|Naka River]] in the northern end of the [[Kanto Plain]]. The {{nihongo|'''Shimo-Samuraizuka Kofun'''|下侍塚古墳||}} is to the north, and the {{nihongo|'''Kami-Samuraizuka Kofun'''|上侍塚古墳||}} is located some 700 meters to the south. The ''kofun'' were excavated in 1692 by order of [[Tokugawa Mitsukuni]], who kept a detailed illustrated records of the artifacts discovered. The artifacts were later placed in boxes made from [[Japanese red pine]] restored to the [[burial chamber]]s, and the site was backfilled. Tokugawa Mitsukuni attributed the tombs to that of the Nasu ''[[Kuni no miyatsuko]]'' named "Ataiide" mentioned on a stele dated 689 AD discovered in a shrine in Ōtawara City, which lists the names of prehistoric rulers from the 5th century.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The Kamisamuraizuka Kofunis the larger tumulus, with a total length of 114 meters. Artifacts included [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirrors]], fragments of armor, cylindrical beads and iron balls.
;Overall length: 114 meters ;Posterior portion: 60 meters wide x 12 meters high ;Anterior portion: 52 meters wide x 7 meters high
The Shimosamuraizuka has a total length of 84 meters, and was surrounded by a moat. Artifacts included bronze mirrors, iron swords and [[Haji ware]] pottery. In 1975, the Shimo-Samuraizuka was [[archaeological excavation|trench excavated]] in connection with a land improvement project and more Haji-ware shards were discovered.
;Overall length: 84 meters ;Posterior portion: 48 meters wide x 9.4 meters high ;Anterior portion: 36 meters wide x 5 meters high
These tumuli are considered to have been built around the beginning of the 5th century AD, with the Shimosamuraizuka being the older of the two. They are located a short walk from the "Sajizuka Parking Lot" bus stop on the municipal bus from [[Nasushiobara Station]] on the [[Tōhoku Shinkansen]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== {{Portal|History|Japan}} *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Tochigi)]]

{{Use mdy dates|date=October 2020}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Samuraizuka Kofun | native_name =侍塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Shimosamuraizuka Kofun 2.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Shimo-Samuraizuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Tochigi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Shimo-Samuraizuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|48|47.51|N|140|7|22.17|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Ōtawara, Tochigi]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kantō region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 4th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Samuraizuka Kofun'''|侍塚古墳 |}} is a pair of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]]s located in the Yuzukami neighborhood of the city of [[Ōtawara, Tochigi|Ōtawara]] in [[Tochigi Prefecture]] in the northern [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. Both received protection as a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 1951.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/215989|title=侍塚古墳 |trans-title= Fujimoto Kannonyama Kofun |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref> The neighborhood has many smaller ''kofun'', including one [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped tumulus]], seven {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=dome-shaped|ja|円墳}} tumuli and one square-shaped tumulus, which are covered under an Ōtawara City Historic Site designation.
==Overview== The Samuraizuka Kofun are both {{nihongo|"two conjoined rectangles" shaped|前方後方墳 |zenpō-kōhō-fun}}. They are located on the right bank of the [[Naka River (Tochigi Ibaraki)|Naka River]] in the northern end of the [[Kanto Plain]]. The {{nihongo|'''Shimo-Samuraizuka Kofun'''|下侍塚古墳||}} is to the north, and the {{nihongo|'''Kami-Samuraizuka Kofun'''|上侍塚古墳||}} is located some 700 meters to the south. The ''kofun'' were excavated in 1692 by order of [[Tokugawa Mitsukuni]], who kept a detailed illustrated records of the artifacts discovered. The artifacts were later placed in boxes made from [[Japanese red pine]] restored to the [[burial chamber]]s, and the site was backfilled. Tokugawa Mitsukuni attributed the tombs to that of the Nasu ''[[Kuni no miyatsuko]]'' named "Ataiide" mentioned on a stele dated 689 AD discovered in a shrine in Ōtawara City, which lists the names of prehistoric rulers from the 5th century.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The Kamisamuraizuka Kofunis the larger tumulus, with a total length of 114 meters. Artifacts included [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirrors]], fragments of armor, cylindrical beads and iron balls.
;Overall length: 114 meters ;Posterior portion: 60 meters wide x 12 meters high ;Anterior portion: 52 meters wide x 7 meters high
The Shimosamuraizuka has a total length of 84 meters, and was surrounded by a moat. Artifacts included bronze mirrors, iron swords and [[Haji ware]] pottery. In 1975, the Shimo-Samuraizuka was [[archaeological excavation|trench excavated]] in connection with a land improvement project and more Haji-ware shards were discovered.
;Overall length: 84 meters ;Posterior portion: 48 meters wide x 9.4 meters high ;Anterior portion: 36 meters wide x 5 meters high
These tumuli are considered to have been built around the beginning of the 5th century AD, with the Shimosamuraizuka being the older of the two. They are located a short walk from the "Sajizuka Parking Lot" bus stop on the municipal bus from [[Nasushiobara Station]] on the [[Tōhoku Shinkansen]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== {{Portal|History|Japan}} *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Tochigi)]]

{{Use mdy dates|date=October 2020}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Samuraizuka Kofun | native_name =侍塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Shimosamuraizuka Kofun 2.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Shimo-Samuraizuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Tochigi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Shimo-Samuraizuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|48|47.51|N|140|7|22.17|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Ōtawara, Tochigi]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kantō region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 4th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Samuraizuka Kofun'''|侍塚古墳 |}} is a pair of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]]s located in the Yuzukami neighborhood of the city of [[Ōtawara, Tochigi|Ōtawara]] in [[Tochigi Prefecture]] in the northern [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. Both received protection as a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 1951.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/215989|title=侍塚古墳 |trans-title= Fujimoto Kannonyama Kofun |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref> The neighborhood has many smaller ''kofun'', including one [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped tumulus]], seven {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=dome-shaped|ja|円墳}} tumuli and one square-shaped tumulus, which are covered under an Ōtawara City Historic Site designation.
==Overview== The Samuraizuka Kofun are both {{nihongo|"two conjoined rectangles" shaped|前方後方墳 |zenpō-kōhō-fun}}. They are located on the right bank of the [[Naka River (Tochigi Ibaraki)|Naka River]] in the northern end of the [[Kanto Plain]]. The {{nihongo|'''Shimo-Samuraizuka Kofun'''|下侍塚古墳||}} is to the north, and the {{nihongo|'''Kami-Samuraizuka Kofun'''|上侍塚古墳||}} is located some 700 meters to the south. The ''kofun'' were excavated in 1692 by order of [[Tokugawa Mitsukuni]], who kept a detailed illustrated records of the artifacts discovered. The artifacts were later placed in boxes made from [[Japanese red pine]] restored to the [[burial chamber]]s, and the site was backfilled. Tokugawa Mitsukuni attributed the tombs to that of the Nasu ''[[Kuni no miyatsuko]]'' named "Ataiide" mentioned on a stele dated 689 AD discovered in a shrine in Ōtawara City, which lists the names of prehistoric rulers from the 5th century.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The Kamisamuraizuka Kofunis the larger tumulus, with a total length of 114 meters. Artifacts included [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirrors]], fragments of armor, cylindrical beads and iron balls.
;Overall length: 114 meters ;Posterior portion: 60 meters wide x 12 meters high ;Anterior portion: 52 meters wide x 7 meters high
The Shimosamuraizuka has a total length of 84 meters, and was surrounded by a moat. Artifacts included bronze mirrors, iron swords and [[Haji ware]] pottery. In 1975, the Shimo-Samuraizuka was [[archaeological excavation|trench excavated]] in connection with a land improvement project and more Haji-ware shards were discovered.
;Overall length: 84 meters ;Posterior portion: 48 meters wide x 9.4 meters high ;Anterior portion: 36 meters wide x 5 meters high
These tumuli are considered to have been built around the beginning of the 5th century AD, with the Shimosamuraizuka being the older of the two. They are located a short walk from the "Sajizuka Parking Lot" bus stop on the municipal bus from [[Nasushiobara Station]] on the [[Tōhoku Shinkansen]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Use mdy dates|date=October 2020}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Samuraizuka Kofun | native_name =侍塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Shimosamuraizuka Kofun 2.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Shimo-Samuraizuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Tochigi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Shimo-Samuraizuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|48|47.51|N|140|7|22.17|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Ōtawara, Tochigi]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kantō region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 4th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Samuraizuka Kofun'''|侍塚古墳 |}} is a pair of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]]s located in the Yuzukami neighborhood of the city of [[Ōtawara, Tochigi|Ōtawara]] in [[Tochigi Prefecture]] in the northern [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. Both received protection as a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 1951.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/215989|title=侍塚古墳 |trans-title= Fujimoto Kannonyama Kofun |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref> The neighborhood has many smaller ''kofun'', including one [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped tumulus]], seven {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=dome-shaped|ja|円墳}} tumuli and one square-shaped tumulus, which are covered under an Ōtawara City Historic Site designation.
==Overview== The Samuraizuka Kofun are both {{nihongo|"two conjoined rectangles" shaped|前方後方墳 |zenpō-kōhō-fun}}. They are located on the right bank of the [[Naka River (Tochigi Ibaraki)|Naka River]] in the northern end of the [[Kanto Plain]]. The {{nihongo|'''Shimo-Samuraizuka Kofun'''|下侍塚古墳||}} is to the north, and the {{nihongo|'''Kami-Samuraizuka Kofun'''|上侍塚古墳||}} is located some 700 meters to the south. The ''kofun'' were excavated in 1692 by order of [[Tokugawa Mitsukuni]], who kept a detailed illustrated records of the artifacts discovered. The artifacts were later placed in boxes made from [[Japanese red pine]] restored to the [[burial chamber]]s, and the site was backfilled. Tokugawa Mitsukuni attributed the tombs to that of the Nasu ''[[Kuni no miyatsuko]]'' named "Ataiide" mentioned on a stele dated 689 AD discovered in a shrine in Ōtawara City, which lists the names of prehistoric rulers from the 5th century.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The Kamisamuraizuka Kofunis the larger tumulus, with a total length of 114 meters. Artifacts included [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirrors]], fragments of armor, cylindrical beads and iron balls.
;Overall length: 114 meters ;Posterior portion: 60 meters wide x 12 meters high ;Anterior portion: 52 meters wide x 7 meters high
The Shimosamuraizuka has a total length of 84 meters, and was surrounded by a moat. Artifacts included bronze mirrors, iron swords and [[Haji ware]] pottery. In 1975, the Shimo-Samuraizuka was [[archaeological excavation|trench excavated]] in connection with a land improvement project and more Haji-ware shards were discovered.
;Overall length: 84 meters ;Posterior portion: 48 meters wide x 9.4 meters high ;Anterior portion: 36 meters wide x 5 meters high
These tumuli are considered to have been built around the beginning of the 5th century AD, with the Shimosamuraizuka being the older of the two. They are located a short walk from the "Sajizuka Parking Lot" bus stop on the municipal bus from [[Nasushiobara Station]] on the [[Tōhoku Shinkansen]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== {{Portal|History|Japan}} *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Tochigi)]]

{{Use mdy dates|date=October 2020}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Samuraizuka Kofun | native_name =侍塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Shimosamuraizuka Kofun 2.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Shimo-Samuraizuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Tochigi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Shimo-Samuraizuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|48|47.51|N|140|7|22.17|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Ōtawara, Tochigi]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kantō region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 4th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Samuraizuka Kofun'''|侍塚古墳 |}} is a pair of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]]s located in the Yuzukami neighborhood of the city of [[Ōtawara, Tochigi|Ōtawara]] in [[Tochigi Prefecture]] in the northern [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. Both received protection as a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 1951.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/215989|title=侍塚古墳 |trans-title= Fujimoto Kannonyama Kofun |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref> The neighborhood has many smaller ''kofun'', including one [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped tumulus]], seven {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=dome-shaped|ja|円墳}} tumuli and one square-shaped tumulus, which are covered under an Ōtawara City Historic Site designation.
==Overview== The Samuraizuka Kofun are both {{nihongo|"two conjoined rectangles" shaped|前方後方墳 |zenpō-kōhō-fun}}. They are located on the right bank of the [[Naka River (Tochigi Ibaraki)|Naka River]] in the northern end of the [[Kanto Plain]]. The {{nihongo|'''Shimo-Samuraizuka Kofun'''|下侍塚古墳||}} is to the north, and the {{nihongo|'''Kami-Samuraizuka Kofun'''|上侍塚古墳||}} is located some 700 meters to the south. The ''kofun'' were excavated in 1692 by order of [[Tokugawa Mitsukuni]], who kept a detailed illustrated records of the artifacts discovered. The artifacts were later placed in boxes made from [[Japanese red pine]] restored to the [[burial chamber]]s, and the site was backfilled. Tokugawa Mitsukuni attributed the tombs to that of the Nasu ''[[Kuni no miyatsuko]]'' named "Ataiide" mentioned on a stele dated 689 AD discovered in a shrine in Ōtawara City, which lists the names of prehistoric rulers from the 5th century.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The Kamisamuraizuka Kofunis the larger tumulus, with a total length of 114 meters. Artifacts included [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirrors]], fragments of armor, cylindrical beads and iron balls.
;Overall length: 114 meters ;Posterior portion: 60 meters wide x 12 meters high ;Anterior portion: 52 meters wide x 7 meters high
The Shimosamuraizuka has a total length of 84 meters, and was surrounded by a moat. Artifacts included bronze mirrors, iron swords and [[Haji ware]] pottery. In 1975, the Shimo-Samuraizuka was [[archaeological excavation|trench excavated]] in connection with a land improvement project and more Haji-ware shards were discovered.
;Overall length: 84 meters ;Posterior portion: 48 meters wide x 9.4 meters high ;Anterior portion: 36 meters wide x 5 meters high
These tumuli are considered to have been built around the beginning of the 5th century AD, with the Shimosamuraizuka being the older of the two. They are located a short walk from the "Sajizuka Parking Lot" bus stop on the municipal bus from [[Nasushiobara Station]] on the [[Tōhoku Shinkansen]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== {{Portal|History|Japan}} *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Tochigi)]]

{{Use mdy dates|date=October 2020}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Samuraizuka Kofun | native_name =侍塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Shimosamuraizuka Kofun 2.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Shimo-Samuraizuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Tochigi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Shimo-Samuraizuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|48|47.51|N|140|7|22.17|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Ōtawara, Tochigi]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kantō region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 4th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Samuraizuka Kofun'''|侍塚古墳 |}} is a pair of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]]s located in the Yuzukami neighborhood of the city of [[Ōtawara, Tochigi|Ōtawara]] in [[Tochigi Prefecture]] in the northern [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. Both received protection as a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 1951.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/215989|title=侍塚古墳 |trans-title= Fujimoto Kannonyama Kofun |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref> The neighborhood has many smaller ''kofun'', including one [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped tumulus]], seven {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=dome-shaped|ja|円墳}} tumuli and one square-shaped tumulus, which are covered under an Ōtawara City Historic Site designation.
==Overview== The Samuraizuka Kofun are both {{nihongo|"two conjoined rectangles" shaped|前方後方墳 |zenpō-kōhō-fun}}. They are located on the right bank of the [[Naka River (Tochigi Ibaraki)|Naka River]] in the northern end of the [[Kanto Plain]]. The {{nihongo|'''Shimo-Samuraizuka Kofun'''|下侍塚古墳||}} is to the north, and the {{nihongo|'''Kami-Samuraizuka Kofun'''|上侍塚古墳||}} is located some 700 meters to the south. The ''kofun'' were excavated in 1692 by order of [[Tokugawa Mitsukuni]], who kept a detailed illustrated records of the artifacts discovered. The artifacts were later placed in boxes made from [[Japanese red pine]] restored to the [[burial chamber]]s, and the site was backfilled. Tokugawa Mitsukuni attributed the tombs to that of the Nasu ''[[Kuni no miyatsuko]]'' named "Ataiide" mentioned on a stele dated 689 AD discovered in a shrine in Ōtawara City, which lists the names of prehistoric rulers from the 5th century.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The Kamisamuraizuka Kofunis the larger tumulus, with a total length of 114 meters. Artifacts included [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirrors]], fragments of armor, cylindrical beads and iron balls.
;Overall length: 114 meters ;Posterior portion: 60 meters wide x 12 meters high ;Anterior portion: 52 meters wide x 7 meters high
The Shimosamuraizuka has a total length of 84 meters, and was surrounded by a moat. Artifacts included bronze mirrors, iron swords and [[Haji ware]] pottery. In 1975, the Shimo-Samuraizuka was [[archaeological excavation|trench excavated]] in connection with a land improvement project and more Haji-ware shards were discovered.
;Overall length: 84 meters ;Posterior portion: 48 meters wide x 9.4 meters high ;Anterior portion: 36 meters wide x 5 meters high
These tumuli are considered to have been built around the beginning of the 5th century AD, with the Shimosamuraizuka being the older of the two. They are located a short walk from the "Sajizuka Parking Lot" bus stop on the municipal bus from [[Nasushiobara Station]] on the [[Tōhoku Shinkansen]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== {{Portal|History|Japan}} *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Tochigi)]]

{{Use mdy dates|date=October 2020}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Samuraizuka Kofun | native_name =侍塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Shimosamuraizuka Kofun 2.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Shimo-Samuraizuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Tochigi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Shimo-Samuraizuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|48|47.51|N|140|7|22.17|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Ōtawara, Tochigi]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kantō region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 4th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Samuraizuka Kofun'''|侍塚古墳 |}} is a pair of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]]s located in the Yuzukami neighborhood of the city of [[Ōtawara, Tochigi|Ōtawara]] in [[Tochigi Prefecture]] in the northern [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. Both received protection as a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 1951.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/215989|title=侍塚古墳 |trans-title= Fujimoto Kannonyama Kofun |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref> The neighborhood has many smaller ''kofun'', including one [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped tumulus]], seven {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=dome-shaped|ja|円墳}} tumuli and one square-shaped tumulus, which are covered under an Ōtawara City Historic Site designation.
==Overview== The Samuraizuka Kofun are both {{nihongo|"two conjoined rectangles" shaped|前方後方墳 |zenpō-kōhō-fun}}. They are located on the right bank of the [[Naka River (Tochigi Ibaraki)|Naka River]] in the northern end of the [[Kanto Plain]]. The {{nihongo|'''Shimo-Samuraizuka Kofun'''|下侍塚古墳||}} is to the north, and the {{nihongo|'''Kami-Samuraizuka Kofun'''|上侍塚古墳||}} is located some 700 meters to the south. The ''kofun'' were excavated in 1692 by order of [[Tokugawa Mitsukuni]], who kept a detailed illustrated records of the artifacts discovered. The artifacts were later placed in boxes made from [[Japanese red pine]] restored to the [[burial chamber]]s, and the site was backfilled. Tokugawa Mitsukuni attributed the tombs to that of the Nasu ''[[Kuni no miyatsuko]]'' named "Ataiide" mentioned on a stele dated 689 AD discovered in a shrine in Ōtawara City, which lists the names of prehistoric rulers from the 5th century.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The Kamisamuraizuka Kofunis the larger tumulus, with a total length of 114 meters. Artifacts included [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirrors]], fragments of armor, cylindrical beads and iron balls.
;Overall length: 114 meters ;Posterior portion: 60 meters wide x 12 meters high ;Anterior portion: 52 meters wide x 7 meters high
The Shimosamuraizuka has a total length of 84 meters, and was surrounded by a moat. Artifacts included bronze mirrors, iron swords and [[Haji ware]] pottery. In 1975, the Shimo-Samuraizuka was [[archaeological excavation|trench excavated]] in connection with a land improvement project and more Haji-ware shards were discovered.
;Overall length: 84 meters ;Posterior portion: 48 meters wide x 9.4 meters high ;Anterior portion: 36 meters wide x 5 meters high
These tumuli are considered to have been built around the beginning of the 5th century AD, with the Shimosamuraizuka being the older of the two. They are located a short walk from the "Sajizuka Parking Lot" bus stop on the municipal bus from [[Nasushiobara Station]] on the [[Tōhoku Shinkansen]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Use mdy dates|date=October 2020}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Samuraizuka Kofun | native_name =侍塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Shimosamuraizuka Kofun 2.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Shimo-Samuraizuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Tochigi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Shimo-Samuraizuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|48|47.51|N|140|7|22.17|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Ōtawara, Tochigi]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kantō region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 4th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Samuraizuka Kofun'''|侍塚古墳 |}} is a pair of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]]s located in the Yuzukami neighborhood of the city of [[Ōtawara, Tochigi|Ōtawara]] in [[Tochigi Prefecture]] in the northern [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. Both received protection as a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 1951.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/215989|title=侍塚古墳 |trans-title= Fujimoto Kannonyama Kofun |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref> The neighborhood has many smaller ''kofun'', including one [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped tumulus]], seven {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=dome-shaped|ja|円墳}} tumuli and one square-shaped tumulus, which are covered under an Ōtawara City Historic Site designation.
==Overview== The Samuraizuka Kofun are both {{nihongo|"two conjoined rectangles" shaped|前方後方墳 |zenpō-kōhō-fun}}. They are located on the right bank of the [[Naka River (Tochigi Ibaraki)|Naka River]] in the northern end of the [[Kanto Plain]]. The {{nihongo|'''Shimo-Samuraizuka Kofun'''|下侍塚古墳||}} is to the north, and the {{nihongo|'''Kami-Samuraizuka Kofun'''|上侍塚古墳||}} is located some 700 meters to the south. The ''kofun'' were excavated in 1692 by order of [[Tokugawa Mitsukuni]], who kept a detailed illustrated records of the artifacts discovered. The artifacts were later placed in boxes made from [[Japanese red pine]] restored to the [[burial chamber]]s, and the site was backfilled. Tokugawa Mitsukuni attributed the tombs to that of the Nasu ''[[Kuni no miyatsuko]]'' named "Ataiide" mentioned on a stele dated 689 AD discovered in a shrine in Ōtawara City, which lists the names of prehistoric rulers from the 5th century.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The Kamisamuraizuka Kofunis the larger tumulus, with a total length of 114 meters. Artifacts included [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirrors]], fragments of armor, cylindrical beads and iron balls.
;Overall length: 114 meters ;Posterior portion: 60 meters wide x 12 meters high ;Anterior portion: 52 meters wide x 7 meters high
The Shimosamuraizuka has a total length of 84 meters, and was surrounded by a moat. Artifacts included bronze mirrors, iron swords and [[Haji ware]] pottery. In 1975, the Shimo-Samuraizuka was [[archaeological excavation|trench excavated]] in connection with a land improvement project and more Haji-ware shards were discovered.
;Overall length: 84 meters ;Posterior portion: 48 meters wide x 9.4 meters high ;Anterior portion: 36 meters wide x 5 meters high
These tumuli are considered to have been built around the beginning of the 5th century AD, with the Shimosamuraizuka being the older of the two. They are located a short walk from the "Sajizuka Parking Lot" bus stop on the municipal bus from [[Nasushiobara Station]] on the [[Tōhoku Shinkansen]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== {{Portal|History|Japan}} *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Tochigi)]]

{{Use mdy dates|date=October 2020}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Samuraizuka Kofun | native_name =侍塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Shimosamuraizuka Kofun 2.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Shimo-Samuraizuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Tochigi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Shimo-Samuraizuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|48|47.51|N|140|7|22.17|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Ōtawara, Tochigi]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kantō region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 4th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Samuraizuka Kofun'''|侍塚古墳 |}} is a pair of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]]s located in the Yuzukami neighborhood of the city of [[Ōtawara, Tochigi|Ōtawara]] in [[Tochigi Prefecture]] in the northern [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. Both received protection as a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 1951.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/215989|title=侍塚古墳 |trans-title= Fujimoto Kannonyama Kofun |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref> The neighborhood has many smaller ''kofun'', including one [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped tumulus]], seven {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=dome-shaped|ja|円墳}} tumuli and one square-shaped tumulus, which are covered under an Ōtawara City Historic Site designation.
==Overview== The Samuraizuka Kofun are both {{nihongo|"two conjoined rectangles" shaped|前方後方墳 |zenpō-kōhō-fun}}. They are located on the right bank of the [[Naka River (Tochigi Ibaraki)|Naka River]] in the northern end of the [[Kanto Plain]]. The {{nihongo|'''Shimo-Samuraizuka Kofun'''|下侍塚古墳||}} is to the north, and the {{nihongo|'''Kami-Samuraizuka Kofun'''|上侍塚古墳||}} is located some 700 meters to the south. The ''kofun'' were excavated in 1692 by order of [[Tokugawa Mitsukuni]], who kept a detailed illustrated records of the artifacts discovered. The artifacts were later placed in boxes made from [[Japanese red pine]] restored to the [[burial chamber]]s, and the site was backfilled. Tokugawa Mitsukuni attributed the tombs to that of the Nasu ''[[Kuni no miyatsuko]]'' named "Ataiide" mentioned on a stele dated 689 AD discovered in a shrine in Ōtawara City, which lists the names of prehistoric rulers from the 5th century.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The Kamisamuraizuka Kofunis the larger tumulus, with a total length of 114 meters. Artifacts included [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirrors]], fragments of armor, cylindrical beads and iron balls.
;Overall length: 114 meters ;Posterior portion: 60 meters wide x 12 meters high ;Anterior portion: 52 meters wide x 7 meters high
The Shimosamuraizuka has a total length of 84 meters, and was surrounded by a moat. Artifacts included bronze mirrors, iron swords and [[Haji ware]] pottery. In 1975, the Shimo-Samuraizuka was [[archaeological excavation|trench excavated]] in connection with a land improvement project and more Haji-ware shards were discovered.
;Overall length: 84 meters ;Posterior portion: 48 meters wide x 9.4 meters high ;Anterior portion: 36 meters wide x 5 meters high
These tumuli are considered to have been built around the beginning of the 5th century AD, with the Shimosamuraizuka being the older of the two. They are located a short walk from the "Sajizuka Parking Lot" bus stop on the municipal bus from [[Nasushiobara Station]] on the [[Tōhoku Shinkansen]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Use mdy dates|date=October 2020}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Samuraizuka Kofun | native_name =侍塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Shimosamuraizuka Kofun 2.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Shimo-Samuraizuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Tochigi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Shimo-Samuraizuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|48|47.51|N|140|7|22.17|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Ōtawara, Tochigi]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kantō region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 4th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Samuraizuka Kofun'''|侍塚古墳 |}} is a pair of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]]s located in the Yuzukami neighborhood of the city of [[Ōtawara, Tochigi|Ōtawara]] in [[Tochigi Prefecture]] in the northern [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. Both received protection as a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 1951.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/215989|title=侍塚古墳 |trans-title= Fujimoto Kannonyama Kofun |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref> The neighborhood has many smaller ''kofun'', including one [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped tumulus]], seven {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=dome-shaped|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} tumuli and one square-shaped tumulus, which are covered under an Ōtawara City Historic Site designation.
==Overview== The Samuraizuka Kofun are both {{nihongo|"two conjoined rectangles" shaped|前方後方墳 |zenpō-kōhō-fun}}. They are located on the right bank of the [[Naka River (Tochigi Ibaraki)|Naka River]] in the northern end of the [[Kanto Plain]]. The {{nihongo|'''Shimo-Samuraizuka Kofun'''|下侍塚古墳||}} is to the north, and the {{nihongo|'''Kami-Samuraizuka Kofun'''|上侍塚古墳||}} is located some 700 meters to the south. The ''kofun'' were excavated in 1692 by order of [[Tokugawa Mitsukuni]], who kept a detailed illustrated records of the artifacts discovered. The artifacts were later placed in boxes made from [[Japanese red pine]] restored to the [[burial chamber]]s, and the site was backfilled. Tokugawa Mitsukuni attributed the tombs to that of the Nasu ''[[Kuni no miyatsuko]]'' named "Ataiide" mentioned on a stele dated 689 AD discovered in a shrine in Ōtawara City, which lists the names of prehistoric rulers from the 5th century.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The Kamisamuraizuka Kofunis the larger tumulus, with a total length of 114 meters. Artifacts included [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirrors]], fragments of armor, cylindrical beads and iron balls.
;Overall length: 114 meters ;Posterior portion: 60 meters wide x 12 meters high ;Anterior portion: 52 meters wide x 7 meters high
The Shimosamuraizuka has a total length of 84 meters, and was surrounded by a moat. Artifacts included bronze mirrors, iron swords and [[Haji ware]] pottery. In 1975, the Shimo-Samuraizuka was [[archaeological excavation|trench excavated]] in connection with a land improvement project and more Haji-ware shards were discovered.
;Overall length: 84 meters ;Posterior portion: 48 meters wide x 9.4 meters high ;Anterior portion: 36 meters wide x 5 meters high
These tumuli are considered to have been built around the beginning of the 5th century AD, with the Shimosamuraizuka being the older of the two. They are located a short walk from the "Sajizuka Parking Lot" bus stop on the municipal bus from [[Nasushiobara Station]] on the [[Tōhoku Shinkansen]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== {{Portal|History|Japan}} *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Tochigi)]]

{{Use mdy dates|date=October 2020}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Samuraizuka Kofun | native_name =侍塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Shimosamuraizuka Kofun 2.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Shimo-Samuraizuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Tochigi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Shimo-Samuraizuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|48|47.51|N|140|7|22.17|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Ōtawara, Tochigi]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kantō region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 4th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Samuraizuka Kofun'''|侍塚古墳 |}} is a pair of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]]s located in the Yuzukami neighborhood of the city of [[Ōtawara, Tochigi|Ōtawara]] in [[Tochigi Prefecture]] in the northern [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. Both received protection as a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 1951.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/215989|title=侍塚古墳 |trans-title= Fujimoto Kannonyama Kofun |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref> The neighborhood has many smaller ''kofun'', including one [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped tumulus]], seven {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=dome-shaped|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} tumuli and one square-shaped tumulus, which are covered under an Ōtawara City Historic Site designation.
==Overview== The Samuraizuka Kofun are both {{nihongo|"two conjoined rectangles" shaped|前方後方墳 |zenpō-kōhō-fun}}. They are located on the right bank of the [[Naka River (Tochigi Ibaraki)|Naka River]] in the northern end of the [[Kanto Plain]]. The {{nihongo|'''Shimo-Samuraizuka Kofun'''|下侍塚古墳||}} is to the north, and the {{nihongo|'''Kami-Samuraizuka Kofun'''|上侍塚古墳||}} is located some 700 meters to the south. The ''kofun'' were excavated in 1692 by order of [[Tokugawa Mitsukuni]], who kept a detailed illustrated records of the artifacts discovered. The artifacts were later placed in boxes made from [[Japanese red pine]] restored to the [[burial chamber]]s, and the site was backfilled. Tokugawa Mitsukuni attributed the tombs to that of the Nasu ''[[Kuni no miyatsuko]]'' named "Ataiide" mentioned on a stele dated 689 AD discovered in a shrine in Ōtawara City, which lists the names of prehistoric rulers from the 5th century.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The Kamisamuraizuka Kofunis the larger tumulus, with a total length of 114 meters. Artifacts included [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirrors]], fragments of armor, cylindrical beads and iron balls.
;Overall length: 114 meters ;Posterior portion: 60 meters wide x 12 meters high ;Anterior portion: 52 meters wide x 7 meters high
The Shimosamuraizuka has a total length of 84 meters, and was surrounded by a moat. Artifacts included bronze mirrors, iron swords and [[Haji ware]] pottery. In 1975, the Shimo-Samuraizuka was [[archaeological excavation|trench excavated]] in connection with a land improvement project and more Haji-ware shards were discovered.
;Overall length: 84 meters ;Posterior portion: 48 meters wide x 9.4 meters high ;Anterior portion: 36 meters wide x 5 meters high
These tumuli are considered to have been built around the beginning of the 5th century AD, with the Shimosamuraizuka being the older of the two. They are located a short walk from the "Sajizuka Parking Lot" bus stop on the municipal bus from [[Nasushiobara Station]] on the [[Tōhoku Shinkansen]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== {{Portal|History|Japan}} *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Tochigi)]]

{{Use mdy dates|date=October 2020}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Samuraizuka Kofun | native_name =侍塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Shimosamuraizuka Kofun 2.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Shimo-Samuraizuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Tochigi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Shimo-Samuraizuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|48|47.51|N|140|7|22.17|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Ōtawara, Tochigi]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kantō region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 4th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Samuraizuka Kofun'''|侍塚古墳 |}} is a pair of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]]s located in the Yuzukami neighborhood of the city of [[Ōtawara, Tochigi|Ōtawara]] in [[Tochigi Prefecture]] in the northern [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. Both received protection as a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 1951.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/215989|title=侍塚古墳 |trans-title= Fujimoto Kannonyama Kofun |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref> The neighborhood has many smaller ''kofun'', including one [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped tumulus]], seven {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=dome-shaped|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} tumuli and one square-shaped tumulus, which are covered under an Ōtawara City Historic Site designation.
==Overview== The Samuraizuka Kofun are both {{nihongo|"two conjoined rectangles" shaped|前方後方墳 |zenpō-kōhō-fun}}. They are located on the right bank of the [[Naka River (Tochigi Ibaraki)|Naka River]] in the northern end of the [[Kanto Plain]]. The {{nihongo|'''Shimo-Samuraizuka Kofun'''|下侍塚古墳||}} is to the north, and the {{nihongo|'''Kami-Samuraizuka Kofun'''|上侍塚古墳||}} is located some 700 meters to the south. The ''kofun'' were excavated in 1692 by order of [[Tokugawa Mitsukuni]], who kept a detailed illustrated records of the artifacts discovered. The artifacts were later placed in boxes made from [[Japanese red pine]] restored to the [[burial chamber]]s, and the site was backfilled. Tokugawa Mitsukuni attributed the tombs to that of the Nasu ''[[Kuni no miyatsuko]]'' named "Ataiide" mentioned on a stele dated 689 AD discovered in a shrine in Ōtawara City, which lists the names of prehistoric rulers from the 5th century.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The Kamisamuraizuka Kofunis the larger tumulus, with a total length of 114 meters. Artifacts included [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirrors]], fragments of armor, cylindrical beads and iron balls.
;Overall length: 114 meters ;Posterior portion: 60 meters wide x 12 meters high ;Anterior portion: 52 meters wide x 7 meters high
The Shimosamuraizuka has a total length of 84 meters, and was surrounded by a moat. Artifacts included bronze mirrors, iron swords and [[Haji ware]] pottery. In 1975, the Shimo-Samuraizuka was [[archaeological excavation|trench excavated]] in connection with a land improvement project and more Haji-ware shards were discovered.
;Overall length: 84 meters ;Posterior portion: 48 meters wide x 9.4 meters high ;Anterior portion: 36 meters wide x 5 meters high
These tumuli are considered to have been built around the beginning of the 5th century AD, with the Shimosamuraizuka being the older of the two. They are located a short walk from the "Sajizuka Parking Lot" bus stop on the municipal bus from [[Nasushiobara Station]] on the [[Tōhoku Shinkansen]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
